discouragedProps.name: slots
discouragedProps.reason: Inherited Dialog escape hatch. Use only when replacing MUI internals is required.

discouragedProps.name: style
discouragedProps.reason: Use only for local layout integration.

discouragedProps.name: className
discouragedProps.reason: Do not use to recreate internal HugoUIModal classes.

actions.notes: Primary and secondary render HugoUIButton. Tertiary renders Link unless renderCustomComponent is supplied.

feedback.notes: messages is used when type is feedback and supports success, destructiveSuccess, and error message types.

customRegions.notes: Use only when the standard title and footer do not match the design.

loading.notes: loading constrains action buttons; showLoadingIndicator controls panel-level loading feedback.

generationPolicy: Use type to choose the modal variant instead of overriding modal colors.
Do not hardcode header, icon, footer, focus, warning, or error colors.
Use buttonDefs to customize modal actions rather than replacing the footer by default.

a11yRules.rule: Modal requires open to be controlled by the caller.
Provide onClose when the modal should close from Escape, backdrop, close button, or secondary action.
A string title becomes aria-label; a non-string title uses aria-labelledby through ModalTitle.
Feedback messages should provide meaningful message text and optional description.

generationRules.rule: Default generated Modal usage should use type="transactional", a controlled open prop, onClose, title, children, and buttonDefs.primary.
Use buttonDefs.primary for the main action, buttonDefs.secondary for cancel/back, and buttonDefs.tertiary for low-emphasis link actions.
For completion messages, use type="feedback" with messages instead of manually composing a transactional modal.

validationRules.rule: Generated code must import Modal from @hugo-ui/mui.
Generated code must pass open explicitly.
buttonDefs may contain primary, secondary, and tertiary entries matching the ModalFooter public types.

examples.name: Transactional modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} title="Transactional" type="transactional" buttonDefs={{ primary: { onClick: handleSave } }}>Content</Modal>

examples.name: Destructive modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} title="Destructive" type="destructive" buttonDefs={{ primary: { onClick: handleConfirm } }}>Content</Modal>

examples.name: Feedback modal
examples.code: <Modal open={open} onClose={handleClose} type="feedback" messages={[{ type: 'success', message: 'Success message' }]} />

type.description: Visual and behavioral variant of the modal.

closeButton.description: Controls whether the modal title renders a close button. Error, informational, and feedback modals are closeable by default.

needsReviewReason: First contract version captures Modal and ModalFooter public structures, but inherited Dialog props are not exhaustively recommended.
